I verified by grep that it has *no* blueprint `\lean{}` pin — it was an iter-001 invention that would create an isolated `lean_aux` node. `K_{p,q}` is only needed to type the headline statements (out of scope this iter); deferred with a blueprint-def-block task tracked for before the headline-statement scaffold.

## What was wrong
`Basic.lean` is still `def hello := "world"` — the foundational scaffold objective was silently dropped in **both** iter-001 and iter-002. iter-002's memory note had only captured half the cause. I read the actual Archon source and found **two** gates:

1. **`_OBJECTIVE_NUMBERED` (the real silent killer):** requires the backtick-quoted `.lean` filename to be the **first** token after `N. **`. The prior `1. **Scaffold \`…Basic.lean\`**` led with the word "Scaffold" → regex captured nothing → plan-validate's "no parseable objectives". (A colon before the filename also kills it.)
2. **No-op filter:** needs a scaffold keyword + the `.lean` name on the **same physical line** (the previously-known half).

## Fix
Reformatted the objective to `1. **\`…/Basic.lean\`** — Scaffold …` — filename leads the bold, "Scaffold" follows on the same line, no colon. **Verified live against the real parser**: it now parses as `(Basic.lean, formalize)` and `basic.lean` is scaffold-exempt, so both gates pass.
